title: plantago triandra subsp. masoniae
intro: Plantago triandra subsp. masoniae, also known as Mason's Plantain, is a subspecies of the Plantago triandra plant, which belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other compounds that contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Plantago triandra subsp. masoniae can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to treat various health conditions.
